hero.title: Make Every Hour of Your
hero.subtitle: Transiting through the UK on your way to the US, Canada, Australia or beyond? This guide tells you everything — transport costs, hotels, attractions, food, and exactly how to spend 8 hours to 2 days.

whoFor.headline: Who Is This Guide For?
whoFor.intro: This is a transit guide — for travelers passing through the UK, not staying. Whether you have 8 hours or 2 days, this tells you exactly what to do, what it costs, and how to get around.
yes: You are transiting through UK airports (LHR, LGW, LTN, STN)
Your final destination is USA, Canada, Australia or elsewhere
You have 8 hours to 2 days between flights
You want to explore London instead of sitting at the airport
no: The UK is your final destination
You have less than 6 hours (stay airside)
You do not have a valid UK transit or visitor visa

visa.intro: Visa rules must be checked before you travel. The rules depend on your passport, which other visas you hold, and whether you plan to leave the airport.
visa.alertBody: Staying inside the airport terminal (airside) follows different rules from going out to explore London. Know which applies to you before you board.

cards.title: Leaving the Airport to Explore
cards.body: You need a Standard Visitor Visa or Transit Visitor Visa. Apply at gov.uk at least 3–4 weeks before travel. Fee: ~£115. Required documents: passport, flight itinerary, proof of onward travel, bank statement.

cards.title: Staying Airside (Inside Terminal)
cards.body: If you hold a valid US B1/B2, Canadian, Australian or Schengen visa, you may not need a DATV (Direct Airside Transit Visa). Always verify at gov.uk before travel — rules change.

cards.title: Electronic Travel Authorisation (ETA)
cards.body: From 2024, many nationalities including Indians need an ETA (£10) even for airside transit. If you have a valid US, Canadian or Australian visa, you may be exempt. Check gov.uk/eta for your specific situation.

cards.title: How to Apply
cards.body: Apply online at gov.uk/transit-visa. You need: passport valid 6+ months, flight details both ways, proof of visa for onward destination (e.g. US visa), accommodation details if leaving airport.

visa.warning: 🔴 Always verify requirements at gov.uk — Vaaradhi Tours provides guidance only and is not a visa service.

budget.title: How Much Will It Cost? Daily Budget Guide
budget.subtitle: London is expensive but manageable. Here's a realistic breakdown of what a day costs depending on your style.
budget.note: All prices in GBP. £1 ≈ ₹107–110. Book attractions online for 10–20% discount.

airports.name: Heathrow Airport
airports.subtitle: London's main international hub — most Indian flights land here
airports.terminals: 5 terminals: T2, T3, T4, T5 (all connected by free rail shuttle)
airports.airlines: All major international carriers including Air India, IndiGo, British Airways, Virgin Atlantic

transport.name: Elizabeth Line (Tube)
transport.notes: Best value. Direct to Paddington, Bond St, Liverpool St

transport.name: Heathrow Express
transport.notes: Fastest. Paddington only. Book online for cheaper fares

transport.name: National Express
transport.notes: Cheapest to Victoria Coach Station. Slower in traffic

transport.name: Uber / Taxi
transport.notes: Good for groups 3+. Uber accepts Indian cards

facilities: Indian restaurants at T2 and T4

airports.name: Gatwick Airport
airports.subtitle: London's second busiest — many European and charter flights
airports.terminals: 2 terminals: North & South (connected by free monorail)
airports.airlines: easyJet, British Airways, TUI, Norwegian, Wizz Air

transport.name: Gatwick Express
transport.notes: Fastest to Victoria Station. No stops

transport.name: Thameslink
transport.notes: Best value. Stops at London Bridge, City Thameslink, Blackfriars, Farringdon, St Pancras

transport.name: National Express
transport.notes: To Victoria Coach Station. Book ahead online

transport.name: Uber / Taxi
transport.notes: Can be slow due to M25/M23 traffic

airports.name: Luton Airport
airports.subtitle: Budget airline hub north of London — mainly easyJet and Ryanair
airports.airlines: easyJet, Ryanair, Wizz Air, TUI, Jet2

transport.name: Train (bus + Thameslink)
transport.notes: Shuttle bus to Luton Airport Parkway station, then direct train to St Pancras / London Bridge

transport.name: National Express
transport.notes: Direct to Victoria Coach Station. Book ahead

transport.name: Green Line 757 Bus
transport.notes: To Baker Street and Victoria. Comfortable double-decker

transport.name: Uber / Taxi
transport.notes: Expensive due to distance. Worth it for groups of 4

facilities: Small selection of restaurants and cafes
Currency exchange (poor rates — use city ATMs)

airports.name: Stansted Airport
airports.subtitle: Budget airline hub northeast of London — Ryanair's main base
airports.terminals: Single terminal (beautifully designed by Norman Foster)
airports.airlines: Ryanair, easyJet, Jet2, TUI, Wizz Air

transport.name: Stansted Express
transport.notes: Fastest and most reliable. To Liverpool St Station. Book online for best price

transport.name: National Express
transport.notes: To Victoria / Liverpool St. Very good value when booked ahead

transport.name: Terravision Bus
transport.notes: Cheapest option. To Liverpool St Station

transport.name: Uber / Taxi
transport.notes: Pricey due to distance. Only for groups

facilities: Airside shopping mall (Caviar House, Harrods to go)

nearby_hotels.name: Radisson Blu Stansted
nearby_hotels.note: Connected to terminal — no transfer needed

transport.intro: London has one of the world's best public transport networks. Understanding it saves you time and money. The Oyster card or contactless bank card is all you need.

oyster.title: Oyster Card — Your Key to London
oyster.deposit: £7 (fully refundable when you return the card)
oyster.where: Buy at any Tube station, Heathrow or major rail stations
oyster.tip: Or just use your contactless bank/credit card — same price as Oyster, no deposit needed. Works at all tube, bus and Elizabeth Line stations.
steps: Pick up an Oyster card at any Tube station ticket machine
Load £20–30 for a day of travel
Tap in AND out at every journey (or you get charged maximum fare)
Daily cap kicks in automatically — you never overpay
Return card at end of trip for £7 deposit refund

caps.notes: Covers Kew Gardens, parts of south London
Use this if going to/from Heathrow on the Tube

travelcards.name: 1-Day Travelcard (Anytime)
travelcards.best_for: Unlimited travel all day including peak hours. Good if making 3+ journeys

travelcards.name: 1-Day Travelcard (Off-Peak)
travelcards.best_for: Valid after 09:30 weekdays, anytime weekends. Best value for sightseers

travelcards.name: 7-Day Travelcard
travelcards.best_for: For 2-day stopovers — cheaper than 2 daily caps

travelcards.name: Visitor Oyster Card
travelcards.best_for: Pre-loaded card delivered before travel. Same as regular Oyster

modes.name: London Underground (Tube)
modes.hours: Mon–Sat: 5am–1am, Sun: 6:30am–midnight (24hr Fri/Sat on some lines)
modes.tip: Use Zone 1 lines: Central, Jubilee, Northern, Piccadilly. Download the TfL Go app

modes.name: London Buses
modes.hours: Most routes 24 hours. Night buses (N prefix) run all night
modes.tip: Bus ONLY accepts Oyster/contactless — no cash. £1.75 per journey, daily cap £5.25

modes.name: Elizabeth Line (Crossrail)
modes.tip: Newest and fastest line. Heathrow to Bond St in 37 mins, to Liverpool St in 44 mins

modes.name: Thames Clipper (River Bus)
modes.tip: Scenic way to travel. Oyster accepted. Westminster to Tower Bridge £6.80, all-day ticket £19.80

modes.name: Santander Cycles (Boris Bikes)
modes.tip: £1.65/day, first 30 mins free per ride. Perfect for sightseeing. No helmet provided

8h.note: Assumes your flight lands at 8:00 AM and departs at 7:00 PM. Clear immigration, store bags, explore, return. Adjust timings proportionally. Always allow 2 hours back at airport before departure.

steps.title: Land & Clear Immigration
steps.body: Allow 60–90 minutes for UK border control — it's often busy. Collect bags, clear customs. Store luggage at airport left-luggage (£8–14/bag, all major airports). Buy Oyster card at station ticket machine.
steps.tip: Do not queue at currency exchange in the airport — rates are very poor. Use a Wise card or get cash from a city ATM.

steps.title: Take the Fast Train to Central London
steps.body: From Heathrow: Elizabeth Line to Paddington (45 min, £5.60) or Heathrow Express (15 min, £25). From Gatwick: Thameslink to London Bridge (45 min, £11). Get off at a central station.
steps.tip: Sit in the correct carriage — signs on the platform indicate which carriages stop where.

steps.title: Breakfast at a London Café
steps.body: Start with a proper Full English Breakfast (£8–14) at any local café near your station. Try Flat White coffee — London's café culture is excellent. Or grab a coffee and pastry from Pret a Manger (£4–6) for a lighter start.

steps.title: Westminster Icons Walk
steps.body: Big Ben → Houses of Parliament → Westminster Bridge → walk along South Bank → Tate Modern (free) → cross Millennium Bridge → St Paul's Cathedral exterior. This 3.5 km walk takes about 2.5 hours with stops and photos.
steps.tip: The walk from Westminster to St Paul's is completely free. Save entry fees for the London Eye or Tower.

steps.title: Lunch at Borough Market
steps.body: London's finest food market (open Mon–Sat). £5–12 for incredible street food — Raclette cheese, salt beef bagels, pulled pork, falafels, fresh oysters, Monmouth coffee. Something for every taste and diet.
steps.tip: Borough Market is 5 min walk from London Bridge station. It's free to enter — just pay for food.

steps.title: British Museum or Tate Modern (FREE)
steps.body: Both are world-class museums with zero entry charge. British Museum: Rosetta Stone, Egyptian mummies, Greek sculptures. Tate Modern: world's best modern art collection in a stunning converted power station.
steps.tip: Skip the queues — go directly to a specific gallery rather than the main entrance hall.

steps.title: Covent Garden & Oxford Street
steps.body: Free street performers at Covent Garden. Walk to Oxford Street — Primark for clothes, M&S for food gifts, Boots for skincare, John Lewis for premium goods. All within 15 minutes of each other.
steps.tip: Primark on Oxford Street is the largest in the world — incredibly good prices for quality clothing.

steps.title: Head Back to Airport
steps.body: Leave the city by 5:00 PM at the latest. Allow the full journey time plus 2 hours for check-in and security. International departures need more time than domestic.
steps.tip: Check your flight's departure terminal — Heathrow has 5. Getting the wrong terminal wastes 20–30 mins.

1d.note: A full day gives you time to see London's best at a relaxed pace. You will need a hotel for the night — book in advance. Check out at noon, explore, check in for your next flight.

steps.title: Hotel Breakfast & Check-out
steps.body: Have hotel breakfast if included (saves £10–15 vs café). Check out and store bags at hotel concierge until evening. Grab your day's transport — buy a 1-Day Off-Peak Travelcard (£14.90) after 9:30 AM.

steps.title: London Eye
steps.body: Book online the night before (£33–38). The 30-minute pod ride gives stunning views across the entire city. Arrive 15 minutes before your timed slot. South Bank area is beautiful for morning walks.
steps.tip: Buy 'Fast Track' tickets to skip the main queue — worth it on busy days.

steps.title: Tower of London
steps.body: A 5-minute walk from Tower Hill station. Entry £34 online. See the Crown Jewels, Yeomen Warder (Beefeater) guided tours (free with entry, every 30 min), the White Tower, and Tower Bridge from the drawbridge.
steps.tip: The Yeomen Warder tour is one of the best free guided experiences in London — funny, fascinating history.

steps.title: Lunch with a View
steps.body: Eat at the Ivy Tower Bridge (£20–30, book in advance) for riverside views. Or get fish & chips from any pub along the Thames for £10–15. Dishoom Shoreditch (£20–30) for world-famous Indian-British brunch if desired.

steps.title: British Museum
steps.body: Walk or take a short tube to Holborn. Spend 2+ hours at the British Museum — the South Asia galleries, Rosetta Stone, Egyptian section, and Greek antiquities are unmissable. The Great Court is architecturally stunning.
steps.tip: The museum shop has excellent gifts — books, prints, replicas — reasonably priced.

steps.title: Hyde Park & Kensington
steps.body: Walk through Hyde Park, past the Serpentine lake to Kensington Palace (free gardens, palace entry £22). The Diana Memorial Fountain is free. In summer, deck chairs and paddle boats on the Serpentine (£12–18/hr).

steps.title: Knightsbridge & Harrods
steps.body: Walk down Knightsbridge to Harrods — even if you don't buy anything, it's an experience. The food halls are spectacular. Nearby Harvey Nichols has a top-floor bar with great views (£8–15 for drinks).
steps.tip: There is a strict dress code in Harrods — no shorts or flip-flops.

steps.title: Dinner in Soho
steps.body: Soho has the best restaurant density in London. Bao for Taiwanese (£15–20), Blacklock for steaks (£25–40), Xu for dim sum (£20–35), or Dishoom for celebrated Indian-Irani café cuisine (£20–35). All within a 5-min walk.
steps.tip: Dishoom gets very busy — join the walk-in queue before 6pm or book months in advance.

2d.note: Two days means you can go beyond London. A day trip to Windsor, Bath, Oxford or Cambridge adds an entirely different dimension to your UK experience.
2d.day1Note: Follow the full 1-Day plan above. Stay at a central London hotel: Premier Inn (£90–130), Point A Hotels (£80–110), or hub by Premier Inn (£75–100) for compact but well-located rooms.

steps.title: Windsor Castle Day Trip
steps.body: 45 min train from London Paddington or Waterloo to Windsor & Eton Central (£14–18 return). Windsor Castle is the world's largest occupied castle — entry £30. Walk Windsor Great Park (free), see the Long Walk. Charming town centre for lunch.
steps.tip: Check the Changing of the Guard at Windsor — it happens most days at 11am. Spectacular and free outside the castle.

steps.title: Bath & Stonehenge Day Tour
steps.body: Organised coach tours from London: £70–90 including both sites. Stonehenge (UNESCO World Heritage, prehistoric stone circle) + Roman Baths in Bath (UNESCO city, stunning Georgian architecture). 10–11 hour day out.
steps.tip: Book via Viator, GetYourGuide or Golden Tours 24h+ in advance. Coach picks up from Victoria or Baker Street.

steps.title: Oxford or Cambridge
steps.body: Train from London Paddington to Oxford (55 min, £14–25) or from Kings Cross to Cambridge (48 min, £12–22). Walk the stunning college buildings (many free), punt on the river (£16–20/hr), visit the Bodleian Library or Fitzwilliam Museum.
steps.tip: College chapels and quads are often open to visitors. Christ Church in Oxford and Kings College in Cambridge are the most iconic.

steps.title: National Gallery — FREE
steps.body: If staying in London, the National Gallery on Trafalgar Square is unmissable and completely free. Van Gogh's Sunflowers, Monet, Rembrandt, Turner, da Vinci. One of the world's greatest art collections in a free public museum.
steps.tip: The National Portrait Gallery (recently reopened) is directly next door — also free.

steps.title: West End Theatre
steps.body: London's West End is the world's best theatre scene. Book same-day discounted tickets at TKTS booth on Leicester Square (up to 50% off, opens 10am). Shows include Hamilton, The Lion King, Phantom of the Opera, Mamma Mia. Tickets £25–80.
steps.tip: Day seats and standing tickets are often available at box offices from £10–25 for even the most popular shows.

attractions.name: Tower of London
attractions.description: 1,000-year-old fortress housing the Crown Jewels, Yeomen Warders, and the world-famous ravens. Free guided tours every 30 minutes. The most visited paid attraction in London.

attractions.name: London Eye
attractions.description: 135m observation wheel on the south bank of the Thames. 360° views over the entire city. Buy online — on-site tickets cost £45+. Sunset is the best time to go.

attractions.name: British Museum
attractions.description: One of the world's greatest museums with 8 million objects. Rosetta Stone, Egyptian mummies, Elgin Marbles, South Asia galleries. Completely free. Open daily 10am–5pm.

attractions.name: Tate Modern
attractions.description: World-class modern art in a stunning converted Bankside Power Station. Picasso, Warhol, Dali, Kahlo. The Level 10 viewing platform has the best free view in London.

attractions.name: Buckingham Palace
attractions.description: The King's official London residence. Changing of the Guard daily at 11am (check schedule). State Rooms open July–Oct for visits. Walk down The Mall for the best photos.

attractions.name: V&A Museum
attractions.description: World's greatest decorative arts museum. The India galleries hold Tipu Sultan's artefacts, Mughal jewellery, and 5,000 years of South Asian art. Closed Tuesdays.

attractions.name: Natural History Museum
attractions.description: The blue whale skeleton and dinosaur gallery are iconic. One of London's most loved free attractions. The building itself is architecturally stunning — Victorian Romanesque.

attractions.name: Hyde Park
attractions.description: One of London's eight Royal Parks — 350 acres of open space. Serpentine lake, Diana Memorial, Speaker's Corner. Hire a paddle boat (£12–18/hr) or just relax.

attractions.name: Tower Bridge
attractions.description: London's most iconic bridge. Walk the glass floor high-level walkway and visit the Victorian engine rooms. The bridge opens for large river vessels — check online schedule.

attractions.name: Westminster Abbey
attractions.description: A UNESCO World Heritage Site where British monarchs are crowned and buried. Poets' Corner contains the graves of Chaucer, Dickens, Hardy. No visits during services.

attractions.name: St Paul's Cathedral
attractions.description: Christopher Wren's masterpiece. Climb 528 steps to the Golden Gallery for panoramic views. The Whispering Gallery inside the dome has fascinating acoustics.

attractions.name: Windsor Castle
attractions.description: World's oldest and largest occupied castle — the Royal Family's weekend home. State Apartments, St George's Chapel (burial place of several monarchs), and the famous Long Walk.

tours.title: Guided Tours — Let Someone Else Navigate
tours.subtitle: Guided tours are the easiest way to see the most in the least time, especially for first-time London visitors.

options.name: Hop-On Hop-Off Bus
highlights: Free Thames boat ride included with some tickets
options.providers: Big Bus Tours, The Original Tour
options.bestFor: First-time visitors who want to cover lots of ground

options.name: Thames River Cruise
highlights: Oyster card accepted on some routes
Best views of Tower Bridge and South Bank
options.providers: City Cruises, Thames Clippers, MBNA Thames Clippers
options.book: Board at Westminster, Waterloo, Bankside or Tower piers

options.name: Free Walking Tours
highlights: Classic London tour: Westminster, Trafalgar, Covent Garden
Dark tourism: Jack the Ripper walks (evening)
options.providers: Sandemans New London, Free Tours By Foot, London Walks
options.book: Just turn up — no booking needed for most
options.bestFor: Budget travellers who want local insight

options.name: Black Cab Tour
highlights: See up to 20 sights in 2 hours
Great for families or groups of 4-5
Driver knows every back street and hidden gem
options.providers: Black Taxi Tours, Guide London (Blue Badge guides)
options.bestFor: Families, groups, or those with limited time

options.name: Harry Potter Tour
highlights: Warner Bros Studio: Great Hall, Diagon Alley, Platform 9¾
Walking tours: filming locations in central London
options.providers: Warner Bros Studio Tour (Watford, 30 min from London)
options.book: Studio tour must be booked months in advance — very popular
options.bestFor: Harry Potter fans of all ages

options.name: London Eye VIP Experience
highlights: The most romantic view in London
options.providers: London Eye (book via their official website)
options.book: Book at least 2–3 days in advance

food.title: Eating in London — A World on a Plate
food.subtitle: London has one of the most diverse food scenes on the planet. Indian food is excellent here, but you should try everything — this city does it all brilliantly.

dishes.desc: Eggs, bacon, sausages, baked beans, grilled tomato, mushrooms, toast, black pudding. The quintessential British meal. Available all day in most cafés.
Battered cod or haddock with thick-cut chips, mushy peas, tartare sauce. Try: Poppie's (Spitalfields), The Golden Hind (Marylebone), Fryer's Delight (Holborn).
Roast beef, lamb, pork or chicken with roast potatoes, Yorkshire pudding, vegetables and gravy. Best experienced in a traditional pub on Sunday lunch.
Finger sandwiches, scones with clotted cream and jam, and cakes. Try: Claridge's (£90), The Ritz (£68), Fortnum & Mason (£68), or budget option at Bea's of Bloomsbury (£35).
Shortcrust pastry filled with beef, potato and swede. Cheap, filling, available everywhere. Great for eating while sightseeing.
Britain's finest dessert — dense sponge in toffee sauce, served warm with custard or ice cream. Order it in any good gastropub.
London's Chinatown in Soho is excellent. HKK, Barshu and Yauatcha (Michelin starred) are top choices. Weekend dim sum brunch from 11am.
Not your typical Indian. Dishoom reimagines the Bombay Irani café — black dal, bacon naan roll, berry pulao, keema per eedu. 6 locations. Queue is worth it.
Edgware Road is London's Middle Eastern hub — mezze, shawarma, freshly baked flatbreads, baklava. Open very late. Maroush and Ranoush are classics.
Great quality, fast, and vegetarian-friendly. Ramen, gyoza, chicken katsu curry. At every major station and shopping centre — reliable and consistent.
Yotam Ottolenghi's celebrated vegetable-forward Mediterranean cooking. Beautiful salads, roasted vegetables, exceptional desserts. Several London locations.
London's greatest food market. Neal's Yard Dairy cheese, Monmouth coffee, salt beef bagels, Spanish jamón, Ethiopian injera, Brazilian coxinha. Open Mon–Sat.
Southall Broadway is the real thing — Punjabi dhabas, South Indian tiffin centres, mithai shops, fresh paan. Brilliant Restaurant and Gifto's Lahore Karahi are institutions.
Banglatown's famous curry mile. Over 40 restaurants competing for business — staff will invite you in from the street. Dal, biryani, karahi, roti all excellent.
Gujarati vegetarian heaven — Sakonis (famous for pani puri, dhokla, dosas), Chetna's, and Ambala sweets. Best for vegetarians and those craving snacks.
Apollo Banana Leaf for Kerala and Tamil cuisine — banana leaf meals, chettinad curries, fresh dosas. One of London's most authentic South Indian neighbourhoods.
London has Michelin-starred Indian restaurants: Gymkhana (1 Michelin star), Benares (1 Michelin star), Quilon (1 Michelin star for South Indian coastal). Extraordinary food.
Southall and Wembley markets sell bhel puri, samosa, kachori, pakora, jalebi on the street. Also Haldiram's, MTR and Deep Foods snacks to take on your onward journey.
London's favourite fast lunch — fresh sandwiches, soups, salads, wraps made daily. Excellent coffee. Vegetarian and vegan options labelled clearly. Everywhere.
Britain's beloved budget bakery — sausage rolls, steak bakes, doughnuts, yum yums. Vegan sausage roll is actually very popular. Hot food from 7am.
Fast food done right — naturally fast food. Superfood salads, rice boxes, wraps. Excellent for vegetarians and healthy eating. At major stations and high streets.
London's coffee culture is excellent. Try independent specialty cafés: Monmouth, Climpson & Sons, Workshop Coffee, or Ozone Coffee Roasters for the best cups.
Marks & Spencer's ready meals, sandwiches and snacks are high quality. Great for airport picnics. All labelled with ingredients — easy for dietary requirements.
Supermarket meal deals — sandwich + snack + drink for £3–4. The British meal deal is an institution. Waitrose has the best quality for the price.

areas.name: Soho & Chinatown
areas.desc: London's entertainment and dining heart. Every cuisine imaginable in 5 square blocks. Chinatown's dim sum, Soho's restaurants, Carnaby Street.

areas.name: Borough Market
areas.desc: London's finest food market under London Bridge. Open Mon–Sat. The foodie destination in the city.

areas.name: Brick Lane
areas.desc: Bagels from Beigel Bake (24 hours), curries, vintage shops, street art. Fascinating mix of Jewish and Bangladeshi history.

areas.name: Shoreditch
areas.desc: East London's creative food hub. Street food, brunch spots, independent coffee shops, pop-up restaurants. Very trendy, young atmosphere.

areas.name: Mayfair
areas.desc: London's most exclusive dining. Michelin-starred restaurants, celebrity chef spots, private members clubs. Dress smartly.

areas.name: Southall
areas.desc: The most authentic Indian neighbourhood outside of India. Take the Elizabeth Line west from Heathrow — 20 minutes.

essentials.title: Money & Currency
items: Currency: Pound Sterling (GBP)
£1 ≈ ₹107–112 (check live rate)
Best exchange: Wise card or Revolut (no fees)
Worst exchange: Airport kiosks (high commission)
ATMs on high streets give good rates
Cards accepted almost everywhere — even street food stalls

essentials.title: SIM & Internet
items: Buy Giffgaff, Three or EE SIM at any newsagent
£10 gets 10GB+ data for 30 days
eSIM: Buy Airalo from India before travel
Airport SIMs are expensive — buy in town
Free WiFi: Tube stations, McDonalds, most cafés
Download TfL Go + Google Maps offline before arriving

essentials.title: Weather & Clothing
items: Famously unpredictable — prepare for everything
Summer (Jun–Aug): 18–25°C, occasional heatwaves
Winter (Nov–Feb): 4–10°C, grey, damp, rarely snows
Spring/Autumn: 10–18°C, pleasant but changeable
Always carry a compact umbrella
Comfortable flat shoes — you will walk 8–15 km per day

essentials.title: Power & Plugs
items: UK uses Type G plugs (3 rectangular pins)
Indian and US plugs do NOT fit UK sockets
Buy adapter at Heathrow Boots (£8–12)
Voltage: 230V — same as India, no converter needed
USB-C chargers work with a simple adapter
Most hotel rooms have international sockets in bathrooms

essentials.title: Safety & Emergency
items: London is very safe for tourists
Emergency (police, fire, ambulance): 999
Non-emergency police: 101
NHS Urgent Care Walk-In (free for emergencies)
Pickpockets: watch bags on Oxford Street and Tube
Download What3Words app for precise location sharing

essentials.title: Health & Pharmacy
items: NHS emergency treatment is FREE for everyone
Boots pharmacy: most useful chain, everywhere
Travel sickness tablets, cold/flu medicine available
Many Boots stores open until 10pm or 24 hours
Prescription medicines: tell pharmacist what you need
Indian High Commission: +44 20 7836 8484

essentials.title: Airport Must-Knows
items: Heathrow: 5 terminals — check yours before leaving city
Allow 3 hours before international departure
US-bound flights: extra security screening (1.5–2 hrs)
Liquids rule: max 100ml per item in clear bag
Airport lounges available with Priority Pass or paid entry
Heathrow T5 has best airport facilities in the world

essentials.title: Shopping & Tax Refund
items: VAT refund (20% back) on purchases over £50 at some stores
Claim at airport customs before checking in
Primark: no tax refund (too cheap to apply)
Harrods, Selfridges, Burberry: all offer VAT refund
Primark Oxford St: world's largest — incredible prices
Boots No7 skincare and Lush cosmetics — popular gifts

tips.title: Allow 2 Hours Buffer at Airport
tips.body: UK immigration queues can take 60–90 mins especially for non-EU passport holders. Always add 2 hours on either side of your city time. Being rushed ruins the experience.

tips.title: Use a Wise or Revolut Card
tips.body: Best exchange rate with zero foreign transaction fees. Load GBP from India before travel for the best rate. Works in all ATMs, contactless payments, and online bookings.

tips.title: Download These 5 Apps
tips.body: TfL Go (transport), Google Maps (offline maps), Citymapper (bus routing), Too Good To Go (discounted food), and VisitLondon for event listings. All free.

tips.title: Book Attractions Online, Always
tips.body: London Eye, Tower of London, and Warner Bros Harry Potter Studio can sell out entirely. Book at least 24–48 hours ahead online — 10–20% cheaper than door price.

tips.title: Walk Between Attractions
tips.body: The tourist map makes London look huge — it is not. Big Ben to London Eye: 5 min walk. British Museum to Covent Garden: 8 min. Walking saves tube fare and reveals hidden London.

tips.title: Never Trust the Weather Forecast
tips.body: London weather changes hourly. It can be sunny at 9am and raining at 11am. A compact umbrella is the single most useful item you can carry. Buy one at Poundland for £1.

tips.title: Go Inside a Traditional Pub
tips.body: The pub is the heart of British social life. You don't have to drink alcohol — pubs serve food, soft drinks, tea and coffee. Sit anywhere you like. Order at the bar.

tips.title: TKTS Booth for Theatre Deals
tips.body: On Leicester Square, the official TKTS booth sells same-day West End tickets at up to 50% off. Opens at 10am. Short queue, big savings. Check what's on at time of travel.

tips.title: Get an Oyster Card Immediately
tips.body: The very first thing to do when arriving at any London station is get an Oyster card or set up contactless. Without it, you pay up to 3x the standard tube fare.

tips.title: Best Free Photo Spots
tips.body: Millennium Bridge (St Paul's view), Primrose Hill (city panorama), Hungerford Bridge (Houses of Parliament), Borough Market (street photography), and Shoreditch for street art.
